nodes.label: P05_T001
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a loyal, habitual Dove user who prioritizes ingredient safety and skin gentleness above all else. They extensively research products through online reviews and ingredient lists, distrusting marketing claims and influencers. While price-sensitive and unwilling to pay premiums for sustainability claims, they're open to switching when presented with doctor recommendations or compelling Indian-specific formulations that address their dry skin concerns.

nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad without clear ingredient disclosure, this person would likely ignore or distrust it, while a product page with detailed ingredient percentages would increase purchase likelihood.

nodes.label: P05_T002
nodes.identity_summary: P05_T002 is a highly brand-loyal, ingredient-conscious consumer who values proven performance over marketing hype. While emotionally attached to Dove due to childhood familiarity and trust in its gentle formula, they're rationally driven by ingredient scrutiny and expert validation. They actively research products through multiple online sources but ultimately prioritize personal experience and trusted recommendations over social proof. Their purchasing is pragmatic—they'll stick with what works unless a clear, validated alternative offers better value or addresses specific skin concerns like dryness.

nodes.label: P05_T003

They are price-sensitive but will pay up to ₹400 for proven quality, showing strong preference for Indian brands and natural formulations. Their risk-averse trial behavior means they rarely switch unless presented with free samples, doctor endorsements, or compelling India-specific claims that promise better suitability for their dry skin.

nodes.label: P05_T004
nodes.identity_summary: P05_T004 is a pragmatic, information-driven consumer who balances brand loyalty with rational decision-making. They are deeply ingredient-conscious and natural-leaning, but not willing to pay a premium for values unless proven effective. Their loyalty to Dove is rooted in childhood trust and functional benefits (gentle, multi-use), but it's conditional on price stability and can be swayed by strong social triggers or evidence-based recommendations. While fun-loving and mood-driven in their daily life, they approach personal care purchases with a skeptical, research-heavy mindset, prioritizing skin compatibility and trusted sources over hype or aesthetics.

nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product concept without a detailed, easily accessible ingredient breakdown, this person would likely dismiss it or actively seek out the information before considering further.

nodes.label: conditional-dove-loyalist-from-childhood
nodes.description: Exhibits strong, habitual loyalty to Dove based on long-term trust, childhood usage, and appreciation for its gentle, lightweight, multi-purpose formula, but this loyalty has clear financial and performance-based limits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Dove only." "Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild, been using since childhood."
[REAL] "Very_loyal" to personal care brands.
[REAL] If brand raises price by 30%: "Look_alternatives."
[SYNTHETIC] Q179: "Stick with the original — it works fine and the premium seems overpriced."
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new ad for Dove, this person would likely feel validated and ignore it, but a competing brand's ad highlighting superior ingredients at a similar price point could trigger comparison research.

nodes.label: trust-pyramid-friends-reviews-dermatologist
nodes.description: Distributes trust hierarchically: highest weight to friends/family and aggregated online reviews, moderate to specific trusted influencers (if perceived as honest), low to generic advertising and unverified expert claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Trusts friends & family (4) and online reviews (4) most; trusts TV ads (1) and packaging claims (1) least.
[REAL] Trusts Ankush Bahuguna: "Honest, genuine, uses few products so clearly not PR, very honest reviews."
[SYNTHETIC] Q111: Allocates trust points: Amazon reviews 3, Mother's recommendation 3, Reddit thread 2.
[SYNTHETIC] Q107: Would buy the body wash a close friend loves over one advertised by a dermatologist.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new ad, a 'Recommended by Dermatologists' claim alone would be insufficient; they would need to see it validated by user reviews or a personal recommendation to consider it credible.

nodes.label: price-anchor-200-350-rupees-refusal-to-overpay
nodes.description: Has a firmly established mental price range (₹200-₹350) for body wash and shows strong resistance to paying premiums for ethical or marketing claims unless accompanied by a clear, proven functional benefit.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Typically spends "200_350" on a single body wash purchase.
[REAL] Maximum premium willing to pay for any value (sustainable, Ayurvedic, etc.) is "0".
[SYNTHETIC] Q72: "Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249. Packaging doesn't justify ₹150 extra."
[SYNTHETIC] Q69: "Stick with my current body wash since the upgrade didn't feel worth double the price."
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium body wash priced above ₹400, this person would likely reject it immediately unless a very compelling functional benefit (e.g., solves a specific skin issue) is demonstrated through trusted sources.

nodes.label: skeptical-researcher-before-any-switch
nodes.description: Displays high risk aversion toward new brands, requiring multi-source validation (ingredients, reviews, trusted recommendations) before switching, even when tempted by discounts or novelty.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Comfort trying new brands: "3" (on a 1-5 scale).
[REAL] Last tried a new body wash because it was free; otherwise loyal to Dove.
[SYNTHETIC] Q114: "Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first" before ordering on quick commerce.
[SYNTHETIC] Q136: Would consider buying a new D2C brand "After it gets listed on Amazon/Nykaa with 100+ reviews."
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a flash sale for an unknown brand, this person is unlikely to impulse buy; they would first exit to research the product, by which time the sale may end.

nodes.label: free-sample-and-doctor-top-switch-triggers
nodes.description: Identifies free samples and doctor recommendations as the primary, low-risk catalysts that could overcome brand loyalty and prompt a trial of a new body wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Top 2 things that would make them switch: "doctor_rec", "free_sample".
[REAL] "Got it for free, otherwise loyal to Dove."
[SYNTHETIC] Q217: "Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size."
[SYNTHETIC] Q231: Ranks a ₹99 mini from a YouTuber review as the most likely trial scenario.
nodes.predictive_value: When designing a launch campaign, including a widely distributed, easy-to-access free sample (not just a discount) would be the most effective way to get this person to try the product.

nodes.label: india-specific-skin-claim-responsive
nodes.description: Shows heightened interest and positive bias toward products explicitly formulated for and marketed to Indian skin types, climate, and water conditions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Interested in a body wash for Indian skin/hard water: rating 5, "yes".
[REAL] Claims that would make them stop and read: "india_specific" is top of list.
[SYNTHETIC] Q229: Would try a body wash with an "ancient tribal recipe from Northeast India" primarily because of the India-specific claim.
[REAL] Prefers Indian personal care brands over international ones due to perceived appropriateness and credibility.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad highlighting 'Formulated for Indian hard water & humidity,' this person would be significantly more likely to engage and read the full product details.

nodes.label: functional-over-fragrance-body-wash-prioritizer
nodes.description: While they appreciate a pleasant fragrance, the primary decision drivers are functional benefits (gentleness, moisturization, skin compatibility), and they would choose an unscented but effective product over a fragrant but ineffective one.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Describes perfect body wash: "fragrance not important, lightweight gentle formulation, cleans and moisturizes."
[REAL] Ideal post-wash feeling: "Moisturized."
[SYNTHETIC] Q246: Picks body wash with simple, pronounceable ingredients (coconut oil, aloe) over one with complex actives at the same price.
[SYNTHETIC] Q250: Would try a fragrance-free body wash but "feel like something is missing."
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating two products, one with a 'luxurious fragrance' claim and one with a '72-hour moisturization clinically proven' claim, they would spend more time investigating the latter.

nodes.label: channel-optimizer-quick-for-urgent-online-for-research
nodes.description: Strategically uses different channels based on need state: quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto) for immediate replenishment of known items, and comprehensive online platforms (Amazon, Nykaa) for discovery, research, and planned purchases.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Uses quick commerce. "If limited selection, look on Amazon or Nykaa."
[SYNTHETIC] Q331: "Pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery when I need it today."
[SYNTHETIC] Q305: Would order from Amazon for preferred brand tomorrow over Blinkit's limited selection tonight.
[SYNTHETIC] Q330: If forced to choose one channel for a year, picks Amazon/Flipkart for selection and price.
nodes.predictive_value: A new brand only available on quick commerce would struggle to gain their trial; they need to see it on a platform where they can conduct their full research ritual.

nodes.label: social-comparison-conscious-groomer
nodes.description: While grooming is for self-feeling, they are acutely aware of and sensitive to others' perceptions, and can be swayed to switch products if they feel their choice is judged as inferior or 'budget.'
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Grooming is about both feeling good about myself and how others perceive me."
[BRANCH] Q64: "I'd probably switch to avoid being seen as using a 'budget' product."
[SYNTHETIC] Q75: Repeats same answer: switch to avoid budget perception.
[SYNTHETIC] Q284: In a PG with others using premium brands, would "feel slightly conscious and consider upgrading."
nodes.predictive_value: An ad that subtly positions a product as the choice of 'discerning professionals' or 'those in the know' would tap into this sensitivity more effectively than a pure functional claim.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-routine-maximizer
nodes.description: Their actual grooming effort fluctuates with mood ('slightly lazy' personality), but they hold an aspirational ideal of an extensive, thorough routine, making them receptive to products that promise an elevated, yet easy, experience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Personality: "Fun-loving, happy, slightly lazy."
[REAL] Relationship with bathing/grooming: "Mood_dependent."
[REAL] Ideal routine: "Extensive routine: thorough shower, hair wash, nail care, face skin care, scrubbing, exfoliating, moisturizing, full body deep cleaning."
[BRANCH] Q63: Willingness to try new things "really depends" on mood.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad for a multi-benefit, time-saving product (e.g., 3-in-1 wash), they would be interested if it promises to deliver aspects of their 'ideal' routine without extra effort on low-motivation days.

nodes.label: packaging-aesthetics-nice-but-not-paid-for
nodes.description: Appreciates natural, minimalist packaging aesthetically, but explicitly refuses to pay extra for it, viewing fancy or designer packaging as a marketing cost that doesn't improve the product.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Prefers packaging that looks: "Natural."
[REAL] Perfect body wash: "simple minimal packaging with ingredients clearly listed."
[SYNTHETIC] Q162: "Skip it — I'm paying for the product, not the bottle."
[SYNTHETIC] Q300: Repeats: "Skip it — I'm paying for the formula, not the packaging."
nodes.predictive_value: A product with beautiful, 'Instagrammable' packaging priced at a premium would be dismissed as overpriced; the same product in simple packaging at a standard price would be evaluated on its merits.

nodes.label: deal-evaluator-value-over-freebie
nodes.description: When evaluating promotions, prefers straightforward price discounts (e.g., ₹100 off) over bundled freebies (e.g., free loofah), and calculates value per ml to determine the best deal.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q91: Picks "Deal B: Buy 1 body wash at ₹259, no freebies" over a deal with a free loofah.
[SYNTHETIC] Q82: "Buy the larger size — better value per ml."
[SYNTHETIC] Q95: Ranks "'₹100 off — now ₹199'" as the most appealing discount presentation.
[SYNTHETIC] Q66: Buys combo pack to save money even if not immediately needed.
nodes.predictive_value: A 'Buy 1 Get 1 Free' offer would be evaluated by calculating the per-unit price and checking if it falls within their acceptable range, rather than being seen as an exciting bonus.

nodes.label: cruelty-free-non-negotiable-if-discovered
nodes.description: While not actively seeking out cruelty-free as a primary purchase driver, discovering that a brand they use tests on animals is an immediate and absolute dealbreaker, trumping all other positive attributes.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Includes "cruelty_free" in certifications that influence purchase.
[SYNTHETIC] Q191: "Stop buying immediately — this is a dealbreaker regardless of product quality."
[SYNTHETIC] Q202: Would stick with a brand even if it partnered with a cause they disagree with, but animal testing is a separate, higher-order violation.
nodes.predictive_value: If a controversy emerged about their beloved Dove testing on animals, they would switch brands immediately and permanently, regardless of price or performance.

nodes.label: multi-platform-review-aggregator
nodes.description: Doesn't trust a single source; cross-references reviews across Amazon, Nykaa, Reddit, and YouTube to build a consensus, and is skeptical of reviews that seem incentivized or lack detail.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Reads reviews on "Reddit,youtube,amazon,nykaa."
[SYNTHETIC] Q113: If brand claims and reviews conflict, they "Search for more reviews on other platforms."
[SYNTHETIC] Q119: If reviews seem fake, they "Check another platform for more authentic reviews."
[SYNTHETIC] Q258: Picks a product with 12,000+ ratings at 4.3 stars over one with 180 ratings at 4.8 stars.
nodes.predictive_value: A product with a perfect 5-star rating but only 50 reviews would be viewed with suspicion; they would seek out the critical 1- and 2-star reviews to understand the flaws.

nodes.label: P05_T005
nodes.identity_summary: P05_T005 is a highly loyal, risk-averse consumer who prioritizes proven efficacy and ingredient safety above all else. Deeply trustful of real-world reviews from friends/family and online communities over marketing claims, they rely on extensive research before switching from their trusted Dove. While they have an aspirational desire for an extensive grooming routine, their actual behavior is pragmatic—prioritizing convenience, familiar effectiveness, and budget management, making them resistant to novelty unless it offers a demonstrable, risk-free improvement aligned with their core needs (gentle, moisturizing, lightweight).

nodes.label: High-ingredient-list-scrutiny-before-purchase
nodes.description: Explicitly ranks 'ingredients' as the most important factor when buying personal care and, when shopping online, looks at 'ingredients' first alongside reviews and brand name.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: A: ["ingredients","brand","price","recommendation","expert","reviews","packaging","fragrance"]
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ["ingredients","review_count","brand_name"]
[SYNTHETIC] I'd choose the detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely immediately look for and scrutinize the ingredient list before engaging with any other product claims or creative.

nodes.label: Loyalty-driven-by-proven-efficacy-and-childhood-trust
nodes.description: Uses Dove exclusively, citing that it's 'lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild, been using since childhood.' Switches are rare and triggered only by high-trust signals like doctor recommendations or free samples.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What body wash brand(s) do you currently use? List all. A: Dove only
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? A: Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild, been using since childhood
[REAL] How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Very_loyal
[REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Repurchase_same
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad for a competing body wash, this person would likely ignore it unless the messaging directly and credibly addresses a specific unmet need their current Dove fails to provide.

nodes.label: Trusts-friends-family-over-marketing-and-influencers
nodes.description: Trusts 'Friends and family' (rating 4) and 'Online reviews' (4) significantly more than TV ads (1), influencer content (2), or claims on packaging (1). Discovery of new products is also led by friends/family.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {"Friends and family":4,"Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)":4,"TV advertisements":1,"Claims written on the product packaging":1}
[REAL] Where do you typically discover new personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["youtube","friends_family","instagram"]
[SYNTHETIC] Go with the recommendation from the person whose opinion I trust most.
nodes.predictive_value: When exposed to a celebrity endorsement, this person would be skeptical, but a campaign showcasing authentic testimonials from 'people like them' or a friend's recommendation would be far more persuasive.

nodes.label: Aspirational-extensive-routine-vs-pragmatic-minimal-execution
nodes.description: Desires an 'Extensive routine: thorough shower...full body deep cleaning' but admits actual routine is 'Mood_dependent' and describes a quick, minimalist shower process (apply body wash, brush teeth, rinse).
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When you imagine your ideal grooming routine...what does it look like? A: Extensive routine: thorough shower, hair wash, nail care, face skin care, scrubbing, exfoliating, moisturizing, full body deep cleaning
[REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent
[REAL] Walk me through your typical bathing routine step by step... A: Apply body wash all over including face (gentle enough), brush teeth, wash hair if needed, rinse with water, walk out
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product concept that promises a simplified, time-saving route to achieving aspects of their 'ideal' extensive routine (e.g., a 2-in-1 cleanser+moisturizer), they would be highly receptive, as it bridges their aspiration with their lazy, pragmatic reality.

nodes.label: Price-sensitive-but-willing-to-pay-for-proven-worth
nodes.description: Typically spends 200-350 INR, finds current Dove's 'price is high', and would look for alternatives if price rose 30%. However, has paid up to 350-400 INR and found it 'worth it,' indicating value is judged relative to proven performance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 200_350
[REAL] What, if anything, frustrates you or feels 'just okay' about your current body wash? A: Price is high
[REAL] If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives
[REAL] What is the most you have ever paid for a body wash? What was it and was it worth it? A: _350-400, yes worth it
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a premium-priced product, this person would need clear, evidence-based justification (e.g., superior ingredient list, clinical results) for the price differential to consider it over their trusted, cheaper option.

nodes.label: Values-natural-as-non-toxic-and-carcinogen-free
nodes.description: Gives 'natural' a maximum rating of 5 and defines it specifically as 'free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin.' Seeks certifications like paraben-free and sulfate-free.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? And what does 'natural' mean to you in your own words? A: {"rating":5,"explanation":"natural means it free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin"}
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Paraben_free,ph_balanced,sulfate_free,cruelty_free
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ["india_specific","intense_moisture","natural"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product ad, claims of 'natural,' 'toxin-free,' or 'free from [specific harmful chemical]' aligned with their health-safety definition will immediately capture their attention and trigger deeper investigation.

nodes.label: Strong-preference-for-Indian-brands-over-international
nodes.description: Believes Indian brands are 'appropriately priced, easy to access and restock' and finds international brands lacking 'credibility.' Unwilling to pay a premium for 'Made in India' alone, but the bias is clear.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Indian personal care brands are performing well Ñ appropriately priced, easy to access and restock. Don't enjoy international brands as they lack credibility
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {"Made in India":"0"}
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the 'Made in India' option at ₹249 over imported one.
nodes.predictive_value: When choosing between two similar products, this person would have an initial bias toward the Indian brand, but final choice would still depend on ingredient quality, price, and reviews.

nodes.label: Multi-platform-shopper-prioritizing-convenience-and-selection
nodes.description: Uses Amazon, quick commerce, and Nykaa for purchases. Uses quick commerce for immediacy but will switch to Amazon/Nykaa for better selection. Ultimately values the wide selection and reviews of Amazon/Nykaa over instant delivery.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["amazon","quick_commerce","nykaa"]

[REAL] Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, use quick commerce. If limited selection, look on Amazon or Nykaa
[SYNTHETIC] Pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery.

nodes.label: Grooming-is-for-self-confidence-and-social-perception
nodes.description: States body odour is 'very important' and that grooming is 'about both feeling good about myself and how others perceive me.'
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Is grooming more about feeling good yourself, or about how others perceive you, or both? A: Body odour is very important. Grooming is about both feeling good about myself and how others perceive me
nodes.predictive_value: Messaging that balances the personal sensory pleasure of a product with its social efficacy (e.g., 'feel fresh all day for you and everyone around you') will resonate more than purely self-care or purely social claims alone.

nodes.label: Requires-clinical-or-expert-validation-for-efficacy-claims
nodes.description: Prefers content like 'expert_review' and 'ingredient_breakdown.' In branch questions, a dermatologist's recommendation is the top purchase trigger. They are skeptical of claims without backing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: A: ["expert_review","ingredient_breakdown"]
[BRANCH] Dermatologist recommendation is most important—I always look for clinical backing and safe ingredients.
[SYNTHETIC] Influencer A with clinical expertise influences me more despite fewer followers.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product claiming a skin benefit (e.g., 'intense moisture'), this person would look for supporting evidence from a dermatologist, clinical study data, or a detailed ingredient explanation before believing the claim.

nodes.label: Desires-fragrance-that-is-refreshing-non-sweet-and-long-lasting
nodes.description: Gravitates to floral and ocean/aquatic scents. Loved a perfume for being 'refreshing, not sweet, fresh, long-lasting.' Wants body wash fragrance to last 'All_day.'
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Floral,ocean_aquatic
[REAL] What specific notes or aspects of its fragrance made it stand out to you compared to others? A: Refreshing, not sweet, fresh, long-lasting
[REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: All_day
nodes.predictive_value: When sampling a new body wash, they will be particularly receptive to fresh, aquatic, or light floral scents described as 'long-lasting' and 'non-cloying.' Overly sweet or synthetic fragrances will be rejected.

nodes.label: Prioritizes-skin-feel-of-moisturized-and-lightweight-over-lather
nodes.description: Seeks 'moisturized' post-wash skin feeling and 'lightweight' texture. Considers lather only 'Nice_to_have.'
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Nice_to_have
nodes.predictive_value: Product demonstrations focusing on rich lather will be less effective than those emphasizing how the lightweight formula leaves skin feeling hydrated and clean without heaviness or residue.

nodes.label: Prefers-minimal-natural-packaging-but-not-at-a-premium
nodes.description: Prefers packaging that looks 'Natural' and describes ideal packaging as 'simple minimal packaging with ingredients clearly listed.' However, is unwilling to pay a premium for sustainable packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Natural
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: ...simple minimal packaging with ingredients clearly listed...
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"0"}
nodes.predictive_value: Aesthetically, they will be drawn to clean, minimalist bottle designs. However, eco-friendly packaging claims will not justify a higher price point unless paired with superior product performance.

nodes.label: Risk-averse-to-new-brands-requires-low-commitment-trial
nodes.description: Rates comfort with new brands a 3 (mid-scale). Top triggers to switch are 'doctor_rec' and 'free_sample.' In synthetic answers, consistently prefers trial sizes or samples before committing to a full purchase of an unknown brand.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 3
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ["doctor_rec","free_sample"]
[SYNTHETIC] Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size to test first.
[SYNTHETIC] Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size.
nodes.predictive_value: A new brand launch will struggle to convert this person without offering a very low-risk entry point, such as a cheap trial size, free sample, or a robust money-back guarantee.

nodes.label: Trusts-influencers-who-are-perceived-as-honest-and-non-PR
nodes.description: Trusts influencer Ankush Bahuguna because he is 'Honest, genuine, uses few products so clearly not PR, very honest reviews.' This indicates a skepticism of over-commercialized influencers.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. A: Ankush Bahuguna (only name recalled), mostly friends and family
[REAL] What is it about Ankush Bahuguna that makes you trust his opinions on personal care products? A: Honest, genuine, uses few products so clearly not PR, very honest reviews
nodes.predictive_value: They will be more influenced by micro-influencers or experts who present balanced, in-depth reviews than by mega-influencers or celebrities who may be seen as paid promoters.

nodes.label: Online-review-reliant-prioritizing-volume-and-authenticity
nodes.description: Trusts online reviews (rating 4) and reads them on Amazon, Nykaa, Reddit, and YouTube. In synthetic scenarios, consistently chooses products with higher review counts over higher average ratings with few reviews, and checks multiple platforms to verify authenticity.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {"Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)":4}
[REAL] Do you read online reviews before buying personal care products? Where? A: Reddit,youtube,amazon,nykaa
[SYNTHETIC] Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? A: Brand A
[SYNTHETIC] Check another platform for more authentic reviews.
nodes.predictive_value: A product with a low number of reviews (even if highly rated) will be viewed with suspicion. They will actively seek out and read negative reviews to understand potential pitfalls before purchasing.

nodes.label: Seeks-products-tailored-for-indian-skin-and-water-conditions
nodes.description: Gives a maximum rating of 5 for interest in a body wash formulated for Indian skin types and hard water, expecting it to be lightweight, mild, rinse easily, moisturize, and be gentle.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {"rating":5,"explanation":"yes"}
[REAL] What specific benefits or features would you expect from a body wash designed for Indian skin and hard water? A: Lightweight, mild, rinses off easily, moisturizes, cleans, gentle on skin
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ["india_specific","intense_moisture","natural"]
nodes.predictive_value: This is a powerful trigger claim. Messaging that explicitly positions a product as 'scientifically formulated for Indian skin and hard water' will immediately capture their attention and create a perception of superior relevance and efficacy.

nodes.label: Values-simplicity-and-multi-use-in-routine
nodes.description: Uses only body wash because it's 'easy to use' and specifically likes that Dove is 'gentle enough' to use on the face, eliminating the need for a separate face wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you currently use body wash, bar soap, or both? What drives that choice? A: Only body wash Ñ easy to use
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? (Top 1-2 things) A: Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild, been using since childhood
nodes.predictive_value: They will be attracted to product concepts that simplify their routine, such as multi-purpose cleansers (face+body) or formats that require fewer steps, aligning with their 'slightly lazy' personality.

nodes.label: Decision-autonomy-in-shared-household
nodes.description: In a shared housing situation, they decide what body wash to buy for themselves, don't know what others use, and keep their bottle in a personal bathroom to prevent shared use.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do other household members use the same body wash as you? Who decides what to buy? A: I decide what to buy; don't know what others use
[REAL] Do you ever notice if the body wash gets used up faster than expected, suggesting others might be using it too? A: Only I use my bottle in my personal bathroom
[SYNTHETIC] Decline sharing body wash; prefer to keep mine separate.
nodes.predictive_value: Purchase decisions are purely individual. Marketing focused on 'family-sized' or 'shared' value packs will be less effective than messaging targeting individual benefits and personal care choices.

nodes.label: Rejects-gimmicky-features-and-seasonal-variants
nodes.description: In synthetic answers, rejects products with 'encapsulated fragrance beads' or color-changing pH indicators as 'gimmicks,' and states they would not pay extra for 'seasonal gimmicks' like a summer variant.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] I wouldn't buy it — sounds like a gimmick.
[SYNTHETIC] Stick with regular variant—don't pay extra for seasonal gimmicks.
[SYNTHETIC] My primary hesitation is if the ingredient would even work since body wash washes off.
nodes.predictive_value: Innovations perceived as purely experiential or novelty-driven (e.g., fun textures, interactive packaging) will be dismissed unless they are convincingly tied to a core functional benefit like better cleaning or moisturizing.

nodes.label: Budget-manager-across-product-categories
nodes.description: When faced with budget constraints across a cart (e.g., body wash vs. face serum), will downgrade the body wash to a cheaper option to preserve budget for the higher-ticket or more specialized item (serum).
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You have two items in your Nykaa cart: a body wash at ₹349 and a face serum at ₹599. You realize you want to cut ₹300 from your cart. What do you do? A: Switch to a cheaper body wash (₹149) and keep the serum
nodes.predictive_value: In a bundled purchase or when managing a monthly budget, they will prioritize spending on targeted treatment products (serums, actives) over basic hygiene staples like body wash, where they are more willing to compromise on brand or price.

nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a new product, highlighting its 'Made in India' origin and value-for-money positioning will resonate far more than emphasizing global heritage or prestige.

nodes.label: defines-natural-as-non-toxic-and-carcinogen-free
nodes.description: Has a specific, health-focused definition of 'natural'—it means free of toxins that could have carcinogenic effects on skin and body—and rates this as extremely important.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? And what does 'natural' mean to you in your own words? A: {'rating':5,'explanation':'natural means it free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin'}
[REAL] Q: Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Paraben_free,ph_balanced,sulfate_free,cruelty_free
[REAL] Q: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ['india_specific','intense_moisture','natural']
nodes.predictive_value: Claims of being 'natural' will be evaluated against this safety-focused definition; marketing must explicitly address the absence of harmful toxins rather than just using botanical imagery.

nodes.label: zero-premium-for-values-unless-ingredient-proven
nodes.description: Unwilling to pay any extra premium for ethical or sustainability claims (eco-packaging, cruelty-free, etc.) unless directly tied to ingredient safety or skin benefit.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0','Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0','Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation':'0','Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'0','Made in India':'0'}

[SYNTHETIC] Q: You see a body wash at ₹349 that claims '100% recyclable packaging' and donates ₹10 per bottle to ocean cleanup. A nearly identical body wash without these claims costs ₹269... A: ₹0 — these claims don't influence what I'm willing to pay.
[SYNTHETIC] Q: A body wash brand runs a 'transparency challenge' — they publish their full cost breakdown... A: Make you think they spend too much on marketing.
nodes.predictive_value: Sustainability or ethical marketing will not justify a higher price point; communication must funnel all value back to the core product efficacy and safety.

nodes.label: lightweight-gentle-moisturizing-seeker
nodes.description: Seeks body washes that are explicitly lightweight, gentle enough for face use, and provide a moisturized post-wash feel, driven by concerns about dry skin.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight
[REAL] Q: What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] Q: What do you like MOST about your current body wash? (Top 1-2 things) A: Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild, been using since childhood
[REAL] Q: What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Dryness
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts emphasizing 'rich', 'creamy', or 'deep-cleansing' textures will be less appealing than those highlighting 'lightweight', 'gentle', and 'non-drying' moisturization.

nodes.label: price-anchored-200-350-with-value-seeking
nodes.description: Anchored to a spending range of ₹200-₹350 per body wash, seeks value within that range, and actively compares prices across channels to avoid overpaying.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 200_350
[REAL] Q: What is the most you have ever paid for a body wash? What was it and was it worth it? A: _350-400, yes worth it
[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're buying body wash on Zepto and notice your usual brand now shows 'Price increased: was ₹249, now ₹299.'... A: Search for usual brand on another app to see if cheaper.
[SYNTHETIC] Q: Allocate 100 points across these five factors... A: Price 35, Past experience 30, Skin benefit 20, Brand 10, Packaging 5.
nodes.predictive_value: A new product priced above ₹400 will face immediate rejection unless it offers a demonstrably unique and significant benefit that justifies breaking their established price ceiling.

nodes.label: multi-channel-shopper-with-quick-commerce-for-urgency
nodes.description: Regularly shops across Amazon, Nykaa, and quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto), using quick commerce for immediate needs but willing to wait and research on other platforms for better selection/price.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ['amazon','quick_commerce','nykaa']

[REAL] Q: Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, use quick commerce. If limited selection, look on Amazon or Nykaa

[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're about to run out of body wash tonight. You can either order from Blinkit and get a limited selection delivered in 15 minutes, or order from Amazon and get your preferred brand delivered tomorrow. Which do you choose? A: Order from Amazon and get my preferred brand delivered tomorrow.
nodes.predictive_value: Product availability on Amazon and Nykaa is critical for initial trial and repurchase; quick commerce is a secondary channel for top-up or emergency purchases.

nodes.label: emotional-catch-rational-close-responsive
nodes.description: Fun-loving personality makes them receptive to upbeat, emotional advertising that catches their eye, but they require rational, ingredient/evidence-based information to actually consider a purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Fun-loving, happy, slightly lazy

[BRANCH] Q: When scrolling through Instagram, you see two ads for a new body wash... A: Ad A might catch my eye first because it's fun and relatable... But I'd only consider buying if Ad B backs it up with solid facts, like ingredients and tests.

[SYNTHETIC] Q: A body wash brand launches a campaign featuring a Bollywood celebrity you admire versus a campaign featuring real Indian dermatologists... A: The dermatologist campaign — I trust expert credibility more.
nodes.predictive_value: A successful ad campaign should use upbeat, relatable storytelling to gain attention, but must immediately pivot to clear, factual information about ingredients and benefits to drive consideration.

nodes.label: pragmatic-packaging-preference-natural-and-functional
nodes.description: Prefers packaging that looks natural and minimalist, but values functional simplicity (like clear ingredient listing) and will not pay extra for aesthetic or designer packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Natural
[REAL] Q: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: Big sustainable bottle with refill bags... simple minimal packaging with ingredients clearly listed...

[SYNTHETIC] Q: Imagine two body washes are identical in ingredients and fragrance. One is packaged in a sleek, minimalist bottle and priced at ₹399. The other is in a basic plastic bottle priced at ₹249... A: Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249; packaging doesn't justify ₹150 extra.
nodes.predictive_value: Overly luxurious or flashy packaging will be seen as wasteful and may increase price skepticism; clean, simple, and informative packaging will be better received.

nodes.label: research-before-any-switch-comparison-heavy
nodes.description: Before switching from a trusted product, conducts extensive cross-platform research comparing ingredients, reviews (prioritizing high-volume reviews), and prices, often delaying purchase to complete this.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q: You're on Blinkit ordering essentials and need body wash urgently... Do you (A) order a familiar brand without further research, (B) pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first... A: B) pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first.

[SYNTHETIC] Q: You have 30 minutes before buying a body wash online. How would you split that time? A: Read ingredient lists 12 mins, read text reviews 10 mins, compare prices 5 mins, watch video reviews 2 mins, check brand social 1 min.

[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're browsing body wash on Nykaa and find two products at ₹349. One has 2,400 ratings with a 4.1 average. The other has 85 ratings with a 4.8 average. Which one do you click on first? A: The one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average
nodes.predictive_value: A new product launch must quickly accumulate a substantial volume of authentic-looking reviews on Amazon/Nykaa to pass this person's credibility threshold and be considered.

nodes.label: doctor-recommendation-triggers-consideration
nodes.description: A recommendation from a dermatologist or doctor is one of the few powerful triggers that can overcome brand loyalty and prompt serious consideration of a new product.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ['doctor_rec','free_sample']

[BRANCH] Q: Rank these five purchase triggers from most to least likely to make you buy a body wash you haven't tried before... A: Free sample sachets are top... Dermatologist recommendation comes next as I value expert opinions for safety.
[SYNTHETIC] Q: A dermatologist you follow on YouTube recommends switching to a clinical brand... What do you do? A: Research more dermatologists' opinions before deciding.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that features credible, Indian dermatologists explaining a product's formulation and benefits will be significantly more effective than influencer or celebrity endorsements.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-grooming-routine
nodes.description: Their engagement with grooming and willingness to try new products is highly dependent on their current mood—more open when relaxed, resistant when stressed.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent

[BRANCH] Q: How does your current mood or situation influence your willingness to try a new body wash... A: It really depends—if I'm chilling and see an ad... I might check it out, but during work stress, I skip anything new.
[REAL] Q: How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Fun-loving, happy, slightly lazy
nodes.predictive_value: Advertisements or promotions are more likely to be effective when targeted during weekend or leisure time, rather than weekday work hours.

nodes.label: fragrance-secondary-to-skin-benefit
nodes.description: While they enjoy refreshing, non-sweet, long-lasting fragrances (floral, aquatic), scent is a nice-to-have secondary attribute that does not drive purchase if core skin benefits are not met.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Rank these factors from most to least important: A: ...'fragrance' is last.
[REAL] Q: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: fragrance not important...
[REAL] Q: What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Floral,ocean_aquatic
[REAL] Q: How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Nice_to_have
nodes.predictive_value: A product concept led primarily by fragrance storytelling will fail; fragrance should be presented as a complementary benefit to core moisturizing or gentle-cleansing claims.

nodes.label: indian-skin-and-water-solution-interested
nodes.description: Expresses strong interest in body washes specifically formulated for Indian skin types and water conditions, expecting them to be lightweight, mild, and rinse off easily.

[REAL] Q: What specific benefits or features would you expect from a body wash designed for Indian skin and hard water? A: Lightweight, mild, rinses off easily, moisturizes, cleans, gentle on skin
[REAL] Q: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ['india_specific','intense_moisture','natural']
nodes.predictive_value: Product claims that explicitly address suitability for Indian skin and hard water will be a powerful hook to capture their initial attention and interest.

nodes.label: skeptical-of-trendy-d2c-unless-proven

[SYNTHETIC] Q: A new D2C body wash brand launches in India... at what stage would you consider buying? A: After it gets listed on Amazon/Nykaa with 100+ reviews.

[SYNTHETIC] Q: Imagine you're choosing between these three body washes at a store... C) ₹499 — organic ingredients, eco-friendly packaging, Instagram-famous D2C brand. A: B ₹299 mid-tier, A ₹149 basic, C ₹499 D2C last.
nodes.predictive_value: A trendy D2C brand will need to overcome this skepticism by emphasizing clinical/test results, ingredient transparency, and accumulating reviews on trusted marketplaces before gaining their consideration.

nodes.label: refill-and-large-size-value-oriented
nodes.description: Prefers large, sustainable bottles with refill bag options for better value per ml, and is willing to buy larger sizes if the per-ml price is cheaper.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: Big sustainable bottle with refill bags...
[SYNTHETIC] Q: What if your favorite body wash suddenly became available only in a 500ml size at ₹549 (previously 250ml at ₹299)?... A: Buy the larger 500ml size for better value.
[SYNTHETIC] Q: A body wash brand offers the same product in three packaging formats... A: Choose pump bottle for daily home use as it’s easy and hygienic.
nodes.predictive_value: Offering larger economy sizes or refill systems will be perceived positively as smart, value-driven options, especially if they align with a 'sustainable' narrative.

nodes.label: free-sample-as-primary-risk-reducer
nodes.description: Considers free samples the most effective and risk-free way to trial a new brand, having used this method successfully in the past.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ['doctor_rec','free_sample']
[REAL] Q: Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand... A: Got it for free, otherwise loyal to Dove.
[BRANCH] Q: Rank these five purchase triggers... A: Free sample sachets are top because I've tried new brands that way before and it's risk-free.
nodes.predictive_value: Sampling campaigns (sachets, mini bottles) are the most effective conversion tool for this consumer, far more potent than discounts or advertising alone.

nodes.label: P05_T014
nodes.identity_summary: A value-driven, ingredient-conscious consumer with deep-rooted loyalty to Dove, stemming from childhood use. They are risk-averse and will only consider switching when presented with a risk-free trial (like a free sample) or a strong expert/friend recommendation. Their purchase decisions are heavily guided by online reviews and a preference for lightweight, gentle, and moisturizing formulations from Indian brands, with a firm price ceiling of ₹400.

nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash ad, this person would likely first scan for the ingredient list before considering any other claim.

nodes.label: price-sensitive-with-a-400-ceiling

nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a body wash making extravagant claims (e.g., 'miracle cure'), this person would likely dismiss it and seek out ingredient proof and user reviews.

nodes.predictive_value: This person would be more likely to engage with and be convinced by ad creative that includes clinical test results, ingredient lists, and expert testimonials.

nodes.label: cross-platform-price-and-info-checker
nodes.description: When shopping, especially on quick commerce with limited info, they will pause and check other platforms like Amazon or Nykaa for better selection, details, and reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? Yes, use quick commerce. If limited selection, look on Amazon or Nykaa

nodes.description: Prefers Indian personal care brands because they are appropriately priced, easy to access, and finds international brands lacking credibility.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? Indian personal care brands are performing well Ñ appropriately priced, easy to access and restock. Don't enjoy international brands as they lack credibility
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the 'Made in India' one at ₹249 because I prefer Indian brands and better price.

nodes.label: highly-interested-in-india-specific-solutions
nodes.description: Shows strong interest (rating 5) in a body wash formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions, and 'India-specific' is a top claim that would catch their attention.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? {'rating':5,'explanation':'yes'}
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ['india_specific','intense_moisture','natural']
[SYNTHETIC] Yes, primary driver is the India-specific, natural ingredient story.
nodes.predictive_value: A product concept centered on solving problems unique to Indian consumers (hard water, humidity) would be highly compelling and stop them in their tracks.

nodes.label: childhood-brand-attachment-to-dove
nodes.description: Has a deep, habitual loyalty to Dove body wash, having used it since childhood, which forms a strong emotional and experiential barrier to switching.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. ... otherwise loyal to Dove. Body wash doesn't give much satisfaction Ñ Dove is light on body and skin, been using since childhood

[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? ... been using since childhood

nodes.label: mood-dependent-groomer

[BRANCH] I'm usually pretty set in my ways with Dove since childhood, and my mood doesn't change that much—I'm happy but lazy, so I don't bother with new things.
nodes.predictive_value: Their receptiveness to trying a new product or engaging with a complex ad might be lower on days when they are feeling 'lazy' or less engaged in self-care.

nodes.label: unwilling-to-pay-extra-for-ethical-claims-alone

Rate each separately: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0','Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0','Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation':'0','Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'0','Made in India':'0'}

[REAL] What is it about Ankush Bahuguna that makes you trust his opinions on personal care products? Honest, genuine, uses few products so clearly not PR, very honest reviews
