nodes.label: ideal-routine-aspirational-vs-actual-lazy
nodes.description: Aspires to an extensive, thorough grooming routine but self-describes as 'slightly lazy,' indicating a gap between ideal self and daily practice.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? Fun-loving, happy, slightly lazy

[REAL] When you imagine your ideal grooming routine â not what you do now, but what you'd love â what does it look like? Extensive routine: thorough shower, hair wash, nail care, face skin care, scrubbing, exfoliating, moisturizing, full body deep cleaning
nodes.predictive_value: Products that promise multiple benefits (cleansing + moisturizing + exfoliating) in one simple step would appeal to their 'lazy' side while helping them feel closer to their 'ideal' self.

nodes.label: no-preference-clinical-vs-traditional
nodes.description: Has no stated preference between 'clinically proven' and 'Ayurvedic/traditional' claims, suggesting they are open to either if the product works.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you prefer a product that is 'clinically proven' or one that is 'inspired by traditional/Ayurvedic wisdom'? Why? No_preference
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing can flexibly use either clinical or traditional storytelling, but must back it up with tangible proof (ingredients, reviews) to be convincing.

nodes.label: prefers-sustainable-refills-if-convenient
nodes.description: In their perfect body wash description, they desire a big sustainable bottle with refill bags, but synthetic answers show reluctance if it requires extra effort or cost.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it. Big sustainable bottle with refill bags...
[SYNTHETIC] Stick with regular bottles; no interest in upfront reusable bottle cost.
[SYNTHETIC] Try it for sustainability but keep a backup of my regular brand.
nodes.predictive_value: They would adopt a refill model only if it is equally convenient, readily available on their preferred platforms, and does not incur a significant initial cost.

nodes.label: dryness-is-primary-skin-concern
nodes.description: Identifies 'Dryness' as their specific skin concern, which directly informs their preference for moisturizing body washes.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. Dryness
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? Moisturized
nodes.predictive_value: Body wash concepts that prominently address dry skin and promise hydration will immediately resonate with their stated need.

nodes.label: P05_T015
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a pragmatic, ingredient-conscious shopper who values natural, non-toxic formulations within a moderate price range (₹200-₹400). They are deeply loyal to trusted brands like Dove but can be swayed by dermatologist recommendations, free samples, or social pressure from peers. They heavily rely on online reviews and prefer Indian brands that cater to local skin and water conditions, while maintaining a simple, mood-dependent grooming routine that aspires to be more thorough.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-checker-first
nodes.description: When shopping for personal care, the first thing they look at is the ingredient list, prioritizing it over other factors like price, brand, or packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: ['ingredients','brand','price','recommendation','expert','reviews','packaging','fragrance']
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: ['ingredients','review_count','brand_name']
[BRANCH] I always skip straight to the ingredients list and reviews – that's what really matters for my skin.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash product page, this person would immediately scroll to the ingredient list and prefer products with clearly listed, natural, non-toxic ingredients.

nodes.label: reviews-reliant-for-validation
nodes.description: Heavily relies on online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa, Reddit, YouTube) to validate product quality and efficacy before making a purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: {'Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)':4}
[REAL] Do you read online reviews before buying personal care products? Where? A: Reddit,youtube,amazon,nykaa
[SYNTHETIC] Check another platform for more authentic reviews.
nodes.predictive_value: When considering a new body wash, they would look for products with a high number of reviews (thousands) and spend significant time reading detailed feedback before buying.

nodes.label: brand-loyal-until-specific-trigger
nodes.description: Very loyal to their current body wash brand (Dove), but specific triggers like a doctor's recommendation or a free sample can prompt them to consider switching.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Very_loyal
[REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Repurchase_same
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ['doctor_rec','free_sample']
nodes.predictive_value: A new body wash concept would need to overcome high inertia via a trusted expert endorsement or a risk-free trial sample to break their loyalty to Dove.

nodes.label: price-sensitive-within-mid-range
nodes.description: Has a defined spending range (₹200-₹350) for body wash, considers price increases frustrating, but is willing to pay up to ₹400 for a product perceived as 'worth it'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 200_350
[REAL] What, if anything, frustrates you or feels 'just okay' about your current body wash? A: Price is high
[REAL] What is the most you have ever paid for a body wash? What was it and was it worth it? A: _350-400, yes worth it
nodes.predictive_value: A new body wash priced above ₹400 would likely be rejected unless it has exceptional, proven benefits that justify the premium in their eyes.

nodes.label: natural-toxic-free-obsessed
nodes.description: Gives maximum importance (5/5) to products being 'natural', which they define as free of toxic, carcinogenic chemicals, and seeks certifications like paraben-free, sulfate-free, and cruelty-free.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? And what does 'natural' mean to you in your own words? A: {'rating':5,'explanation':'natural means it free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin'}
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Paraben_free,ph_balanced,sulfate_free,cruelty_free
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ['natural']
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that highlights 'free from' toxins, carcinogens, and specific harsh chemicals will immediately capture their attention and drive deeper investigation.

nodes.label: indian-brand-preferrer-for-value-access
nodes.description: Prefers Indian personal care brands over international ones, citing better pricing, easier access, and a perception that international brands lack credibility for the Indian context.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Indian personal care brands are performing well Ñ appropriately priced, easy to access and restock. Don't enjoy international brands as they lack credibility
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the 'Made in India' option at ₹249.
nodes.predictive_value: A new body wash brand that is Indian and markets itself as formulated for Indian skin/water conditions will have a higher initial appeal than an unknown international brand.

nodes.label: trusts-friends-family-over-influencers-experts
nodes.description: Trusts recommendations from friends and family the most, places moderate trust in online reviews, and has low trust in influencers, dermatologists, and traditional advertising.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: {'Friends and family':4, 'Instagram or YouTube influencers':2, 'Dermatologists or skin doctors':2, 'TV advertisements':1}
[REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. A: Ankush Bahuguna (only name recalled), mostly friends and family
nodes.predictive_value: A personal recommendation from a close friend will be more influential in triggering a trial than a paid influencer campaign or a clinical dermatologist claim.

nodes.label: lightweight-gentle-moisturizing-seeker
nodes.description: Prioritizes a lightweight, gentle formulation that cleans without stripping, can be used on the face, and leaves skin feeling moisturized, not tight.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? (Top 1-2 things) A: Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild
[REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
nodes.predictive_value: A product concept that emphasizes a 'lightweight gel', 'gentle enough for face', and 'intense moisture' will strongly align with their core needs.

nodes.label: fragrance-appreciates-refreshing-but-not-driver
nodes.description: While fragrance is not a primary purchase driver (ranked last), they appreciate refreshing, not-too-sweet, fresh, and long-lasting scents like ocean aquatic or floral.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: ...'fragrance' [last]
[REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Floral,ocean_aquatic
[REAL] What specific notes or aspects of its fragrance made it stand out to you compared to others? A: Refreshing, not sweet, fresh, long-lasting
nodes.predictive_value: An ad focusing solely on fragrance will not resonate, but if the product checks other boxes, a refreshing, long-lasting scent profile will be a valued bonus.

nodes.label: convenience-driven-simple-routine
nodes.description: Uses body wash exclusively for its ease of use, follows a quick, simple shower routine, and finds extensive grooming routines mood-dependent rather than daily practice.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you currently use body wash, bar soap, or both? What drives that choice? A: Only body wash Ñ easy to use
[REAL] Walk me through your typical bathing routine step by step... A: Apply body wash all over including face (gentle enough), brush teeth, wash hair if needed, rinse with water, walk out
[REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent
nodes.predictive_value: They would reject body wash formats that add steps or complexity (e.g., multi-step routines, powders to mix) in favor of simple, all-in-one cleansers.

nodes.label: sustainability-appreciates-but-wont-pay-premium
nodes.description: Values sustainable elements like refill packs and eco-friendly packaging in their ideal product vision, but is not willing to pay any price premium for them.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0'}
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: Big sustainable bottle with refill bags...
nodes.predictive_value: A sustainable body wash will only be chosen if it is priced the same or less than a conventional alternative; they will not pay extra for the eco-benefit alone.

nodes.label: multi-channel-shopper-context-dependent
nodes.description: Shops across Amazon, Nykaa, and quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto), choosing the channel based on urgency, selection, and price in the moment.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: ['amazon','quick_commerce','nykaa']

[REAL] Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, use quick commerce. If limited selection, look on Amazon or Nykaa
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Zepto for instant delivery despite the higher price.
nodes.predictive_value: For immediate need, they'll buy from quick commerce even at a slight premium; for planned purchases or research, they'll use Amazon/Nykaa for wider selection and reviews.

nodes.label: risk-averse-tries-new-via-free-small-samples
nodes.description: Moderately comfortable (3/5) trying new brands, but strongly prefers to mitigate risk through free samples, trial sizes, or sachets before committing to a full-size purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 3
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ['free_sample']
[SYNTHETIC] Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size.
nodes.predictive_value: A new brand's most effective conversion tactic would be to offer a free or very low-cost (₹99) trial size, as a full-price commitment would be a barrier.

nodes.label: socially-conscious-about-body-odour-perception
nodes.description: Views grooming as being both for self-feeling and how others perceive them, citing body odour as 'very important'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Is grooming more about feeling good yourself, or about how others perceive you, or both? A: Body odour is very important. Grooming is about both feeling good about myself and how others perceive me
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that ties the product to social confidence, positive first impressions, or avoiding negative perception due to odour will resonate deeply.

nodes.label: skin-concern-dryness-moisture-focused
nodes.description: Has a specific skin concern of dryness, making moisturizing efficacy a key benefit they look for in a body wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Dryness
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ['intense_moisture']
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash claiming 'intense moisture', '24h hydration', or 'for dry skin' will immediately stand out and prompt further investigation.

nodes.label: peer-comparison-upgrades-to-avoid-budget-label
nodes.description: Susceptible to social pressure from peers and colleagues; may switch from a satisfactory product if it's perceived as a 'budget option' to fit in or avoid judgment.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd probably switch to avoid being seen as using a 'budget' product. Even though I was satisfied, my colleague's comment made me self-conscious about grooming.
[SYNTHETIC] I’d probably switch to avoid being seen as using a 'budget' product.
nodes.predictive_value: In a social or workplace setting where grooming is perceived as a status marker, they might be persuaded to trade up to a more premium brand to maintain social standing.

nodes.label: emotional-ad-responsive-over-clinical
nodes.description: More likely to stop and engage with an ad that tells an emotional, uplifting story about feeling confident and renewed, rather than a clinical ingredient-focused ad.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Ad A would grab my attention for sure. I love stories that make me feel good and refreshed... the emotional connection – feeling confident and happy – makes me want to try it more than just listing ingredients.
nodes.predictive_value: A video ad with a heartwarming narrative, uplifting music, and visuals of renewal will be more effective at capturing their attention than a fact-based, ingredient-led ad.

nodes.label: expert-ingredient-breakdown-seeker
nodes.description: Actively seeks out detailed expert reviews and ingredient breakdowns (not just opinions) as trusted content to inform purchase decisions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: A: Expert_review,ingredient_breakdown
[SYNTHETIC] 15-minute deep dive covering ingredients, pH testing, and comparison with alternatives.
nodes.predictive_value: They would be highly engaged by a brand's detailed blog post or video explaining each ingredient's function, especially for Indian skin.

nodes.label: no-subscription-preference-for-body-wash
nodes.description: Prefers one-time purchases and dislikes subscription models for body wash, valuing the freedom to switch brands or skip purchases as needed.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Choose the one-time purchase at ₹349. I don’t like subscriptions for body wash.
[SYNTHETIC] Don't subscribe — I like the freedom to switch brands anytime.
nodes.predictive_value: They would reject a body wash subscription offer, even with a discount, in favor of maintaining control over when and what they buy.

nodes.label: india-specific-skin-hard-water-claim-responsive
nodes.description: Highly interested (5/5) in body washes specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions, expecting lightweight, mild, easy-rinse benefits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {'rating':5,'explanation':'yes'}
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ['india_specific']
nodes.predictive_value: A product headline or claim like 'Formulated for Indian Skin & Hard Water' would be a powerful hook, making them stop and explore further.

nodes.label: past-experience-weighted-decision-anchor
nodes.description: Heavily weights past positive experience (e.g., using Dove since childhood) as a key decision anchor, making established trust hard to override.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand... A: ...otherwise loyal to Dove. Body wash doesn't give much satisfaction Ñ Dove is light on body and skin, been using since childhood
[SYNTHETIC] Allocate 100 points across these five factors... Past experience with the product: 30
nodes.predictive_value: A new product will be judged against the benchmark of their long-term experience with Dove; it must clearly outperform on key attributes to justify a switch.

nodes.label: limited-time-flash-sale-responsive
nodes.description: Finds limited-time flash sales (e.g., '50% off on Nykaa') highly exciting and motivating, ranking them as the top purchase trigger for new products.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd rank the 50% off flash sale first because I get excited about savings and it feels like a no-brainer.
[SYNTHETIC] '₹100 off — now ₹199' ranked as most appealing discount.
nodes.predictive_value: A time-sensitive, deep discount offer (e.g., 'Sale ends tonight') would be the most effective way to drive an impulsive trial of a new body wash.

nodes.label: bestseller-popularity-tag-trusts
nodes.description: Trusts 'Bestseller' tags on e-commerce platforms as a signal of product reliability and social proof, often checking them after flash sales.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Then the Bestseller tag since it suggests popularity and reliability.
[SYNTHETIC] Search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto.
nodes.predictive_value: A 'Bestseller' or 'Amazon's Choice' badge on a product listing would increase their likelihood of clicking and considering it.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-open-but-no-price-premium
nodes.description: Has no preference between clinically proven and Ayurvedic products, is open to trying Ayurvedic brands, but is not willing to pay a premium for them.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you prefer a product that is 'clinically proven' or one that is 'inspired by traditional/Ayurvedic wisdom'? Why? A: No_preference
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... 'Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0'
nodes.predictive_value: An Ayurvedic body wash will be considered if it is competitively priced with mainstream options and has good reviews, but not if marketed as a premium niche product.

nodes.label: cruelty-free-preference-no-extra-pay
nodes.description: Prefers cruelty-free products and includes it as an influencing certification, but, like other values, is not willing to pay extra for it.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: ...cruelty_free
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... 'Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'0'
nodes.predictive_value: A cruelty-free claim is a nice-to-have that can tip the balance between two otherwise equal products, but will not justify a higher price point.

nodes.label: avoids-sharing-personal-body-wash
nodes.description: Prefers to keep body wash for personal use only, does not share with household members, and would decline sharing with roommates even to save money.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do other household members use the same body wash as you? Who decides what to buy? A: I decide what to buy; don't know what others use
[SYNTHETIC] Decline — I don't want to share my body wash.
nodes.predictive_value: They would not be interested in 'family size' or 'shared household' product concepts, preferring individual ownership and use.

nodes.label: ideal-routine-aspirational-vs-actual-simple
nodes.description: Harbors an aspirational ideal of an extensive, thorough grooming routine, but in reality follows a quick, simple routine, indicating a gap between aspiration and daily habit.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When you imagine your ideal grooming routine â not what you do now, but what you'd love â what does it look like? A: Extensive routine: thorough shower, hair wash, nail care, face skin care, scrubbing, exfoliating, moisturizing, full body deep cleaning
[REAL] Walk me through your typical bathing routine step by step... A: Apply body wash all over including face... rinse with water, walk out
nodes.predictive_value: They might be attracted to multi-benefit or '2-in-1' products that promise a step toward their ideal routine without adding time or complexity to their actual routine.

nodes.label: dislikes-fancy-ineffective-overpriced-products
nodes.description: Has a negative perception of many body washes as being unnecessarily expensive, fancy, and not really working, often containing harsh chemicals.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you categorize body washes in your mind? What makes one different from another? A: Has chemicals, harsh on skin, unnecessarily expensive, fancy but doesn't really work
nodes.predictive_value: They will be skeptical of overly luxurious packaging, celebrity endorsements, or vague 'premium' claims unless backed by clear ingredient efficacy and reasonable pricing.

nodes.label: prefers-simple-minimal-ingredient-clear-packaging
nodes.description: Prefers natural-looking, simple, minimal packaging where ingredients are clearly listed, aligning with a desire for transparency and avoiding 'fancy' designs.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Natural
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: ...simple minimal packaging with ingredients clearly listed...
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash with clean, minimalist, and informative packaging (highlighting key ingredients/claims) will be perceived as more trustworthy and aligned with their values than ornate packaging.

nodes.label: trusts-honest-influencers-with-few-promotions
nodes.description: Trusts specific influencers (like Ankush Bahuguna) perceived as honest and genuine because they use few products, making their recommendations seem less like paid promotions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is it about Ankush Bahuguna that makes you trust his opinions on personal care products? A: Honest, genuine, uses few products so clearly not PR, very honest reviews
nodes.predictive_value: An influencer campaign using creators known for selective, honest reviews would be more credible than one using mainstream celebrities or influencers with many sponsorships.

nodes.label: value-maximizer-opts-for-larger-size-if-cheaper-per-ml
nodes.description: Will choose a larger package size if it offers a better value per ml, even if the upfront cost is higher, to get more for their money over time.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Buy the larger size — better value per ml.
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the mega pack from brand website for better price.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with size options, they will calculate and likely choose the larger size if the per-unit cost is lower, assuming they have storage and trust the product.

nodes.label: animal-testing-dealbreaker-post-discovery
nodes.description: Considers animal testing a non-negotiable dealbreaker; would stop buying a brand immediately upon discovering they test on animals, regardless of product quality.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Stop buying immediately — animal testing is a dealbreaker regardless of product quality.
nodes.predictive_value: If a brand they use is exposed for animal testing, they would switch away decisively, even if it means leaving a beloved product.

nodes.label: P05_T016
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a highly loyal Dove user since childhood, whose purchase decisions are driven by a deep need for ingredient transparency and safety, prioritizing 'natural' as toxic-free. They rely heavily on online reviews and trusted personal networks, and while resistant to change, can be swayed by dermatologist recommendations or free samples. Price-conscious but not cheap, they compare options across platforms and will switch if their trusted brand raises prices.

nodes.label: dove-loyal-since-childhood
nodes.description: Extremely loyal to Dove body wash, having used it since childhood and citing it as lightweight, gentle on face, and effective.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q23: 'loyal to Dove. Body wash doesn't give much satisfaction – Dove is light on body and skin, been using since childhood.'
[REAL] Q43: 'Dove only.'
[REAL] Q44: 'Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild, been using since childhood.'
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely compare it to Dove's lightweight, gentle formula and default to Dove unless given a compelling reason to switch.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-first-shopper
nodes.description: When shopping online, looks at the ingredients list before anything else.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q24: 'ingredients' is first of top 3 when shopping online.
[BRANCH] Q62: 'I always skip straight to the ingredients list and reviews – that's what really matters for my skin.'
[SYNTHETIC] Q112: Chooses detailed ingredient breakdown over a brand video.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad or product page, this person would likely scroll directly to the ingredient list before considering any other claims or imagery.

nodes.label: online-review-dependent
nodes.description: Heavily relies on online reviews from platforms like Amazon and Nykaa to validate product claims and safety.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: Trusts online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa) 4/5.
[REAL] Q59: Reads reviews on reddit, youtube, amazon, nykaa.
[SYNTHETIC] Q94: Trusts product with 15,000 reviews over one with 120 reviews despite lower rating.
nodes.predictive_value: A new body wash with a high volume of positive reviews on Amazon or Nykaa would significantly increase this person's likelihood of purchase.

nodes.label: friends-family-trusted-source
nodes.description: Values recommendations from friends and family more than any other source, including influencers.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: Trusts friends and family 4/5.
[REAL] Q54: Discovers new products through friends_family.
[SYNTHETIC] Q107: Chooses a close friend's recommendation over a dermatologist-featured ad.
nodes.predictive_value: A personal, casual recommendation from a friend or family member would be a powerful trigger for trying a new body wash.

nodes.label: free-sample-triggered-switcher
nodes.description: Free samples are a top trigger for trying new brands because they offer a risk-free trial.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q22: 'free_sample' is a top 3 switch trigger.
[REAL] Q23: 'Got it for free, otherwise loyal to Dove.'
[BRANCH] Q60: 'Free sample sachets are top because I've tried new brands that way before and it's risk-free.'
nodes.predictive_value: Offering free sample sachets would be an extremely effective way to get this person to trial a new body wash brand.

nodes.label: doctor-recommendation-triggered
nodes.description: A recommendation from a dermatologist or doctor is a top trigger for switching from a trusted brand.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q22: 'doctor_rec' is a top 3 switch trigger.
[BRANCH] Q60: 'Dermatologist recommendation comes next as I value expert opinions for safety.'
nodes.predictive_value: An ad or YouTube video featuring a credible dermatologist recommending a body wash would capture attention and prompt consideration.

nodes.label: price-increase-alternative-seeker
nodes.description: If the price of a favored brand increases significantly (e.g., 30%), will actively look for alternatives.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q26: If a brand raised its price by 30%, 'Look_alternatives.'
[SYNTHETIC] Q71: If price increased on Zepto, 'Search for my usual brand on another app to see if it's cheaper there.'
nodes.predictive_value: A price increase on Dove would lead this person to research and potentially switch to a competitively priced alternative.

nodes.label: natural-toxic-free-focused
nodes.description: Defines 'natural' as free of all toxics and carcinogens, rating this importance as 5/5.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q12: 'natural means it free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin.' Rating: 5.
[REAL] Q50: 'natural' is a top claim that makes them stop and read.
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash marketed as 'natural' with clear evidence of being toxic-free would strongly attract this person's attention.

nodes.label: certification-sensitive
nodes.description: Specifically influenced by certifications like paraben-free, pH balanced, sulfate-free, and cruelty-free.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q13: Selects 'Paraben_free, ph_balanced, sulfate_free, cruelty_free.'
[SYNTHETIC] Q110: 'Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list' when seeing a 'Paraben-Free' claim.
nodes.predictive_value: Displaying these specific certifications prominently on packaging or in ads would build immediate trust and consideration.

nodes.label: indian-brand-preferrer
nodes.description: Prefers Indian personal care brands for being appropriately priced, easy to access, and credible, while distrusting international brands.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q14: 'Indian personal care brands are performing well – appropriately priced, easy to access and restock. Don't enjoy international brands as they lack credibility.'
[SYNTHETIC] Q83: Buys the 'Made in India' option over an imported one at the same price.
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash marketed as an Indian brand with ingredients sourced from India would resonate more than an international luxury brand.

nodes.label: no-premium-for-values
nodes.description: Unwilling to pay any premium (%) for values like sustainability, Ayurvedic, clinical-grade, cruelty-free, or 'Made in India'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q15: Premium 0% for all: 'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging', 'Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients', 'Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation', 'Cruelty-free, not tested on animals', 'Made in India'.
nodes.predictive_value: Despite valuing natural and certifications, a body wash must be competitively priced; added value claims cannot justify a higher price point.

nodes.label: lightweight-texture-preferrer
nodes.description: Strongly prefers a lightweight texture in body wash, which is a key reason for liking Dove.

[REAL] Q10: Ideal routine is extensive but current is minimal.

[REAL] Q50: 'intense_moisture' is a top stopping claim.

[REAL] Q51: Most paid '350-400, yes worth it.'

nodes.label: loyalty-with-exceptions

[REAL] Q23: Loyal to Dove unless got a free sample.

nodes.label: ad-avoidant-trusts-reviews

[SYNTHETIC] Q331: Picks Zepto for instant delivery when needed today.
nodes.predictive_value: This person will fluidly move between online channels; being available on Amazon and Nykaa is critical, with quick commerce for top-up.

nodes.label: risk-averse-trier
nodes.description: Rates comfort with new brands as 3/5 and prefers low-risk trial methods (samples, trusted recs) before committing.

[BRANCH] Q60: Free samples are 'risk-free'.
[SYNTHETIC] Q212: Would only try a new brand after a bad experience if they offer a trial size.
nodes.predictive_value: New brand launches should offer low-commitment trial sizes or strong money-back guarantees to encourage trial from this cautious consumer.

nodes.label: fact-over-emotion-ad-responder
nodes.description: More likely to stop and engage with an ad that lists ingredients, clinical test results, and expert clips than one with an emotional story.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q61: 'I'd definitely stop for Ad B. I need to see the facts – ingredients like pH-balanced and sulfate-free tell me it's safe and effective.'
nodes.predictive_value: Advertising creative that leads with factual, ingredient-focused claims and expert credibility will be more effective than emotional or lifestyle-focused narratives.

nodes.label: slightly-lazy-researcher

[BRANCH] Q63: 'I'm happy but lazy, so I don't bother with new things.'
nodes.predictive_value: While they value information, their laziness means purchase triggers need to be easy and low-effort (e.g., a friend's direct recommendation, a prominent 'best for dry skin' tag).

nodes.label: shared-housing-independent-buyer
nodes.description: Lives in shared housing but makes independent purchasing decisions for personal care and does not share products.

[REAL] Q33: 'Only I use my bottle in my personal bathroom.'

nodes.label: body-odour-conscious
nodes.description: States that body odour is very important and grooming is about both feeling good oneself and how others perceive you.

nodes.predictive_value: Claims related to long-lasting freshness, odor protection, or social confidence would align with this dual motivation for grooming.

nodes.label: P05_T017
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a cautious yet curious mid-range buyer who balances brand loyalty with careful ingredient scrutiny. They rely heavily on trusted personal networks (friends/family) and detailed online reviews for validation, while being skeptical of marketing claims unless backed by evidence. Their purchase decisions are driven by a strong preference for Indian brands, lightweight/mild formulations suitable for their dry skin, and practical value—rarely paying premiums for sustainability or certifications alone.

nodes.label: ingredient-label-scrutinizer
nodes.description: Always checks ingredient lists first when evaluating a product, both online and on physical packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: ['ingredients','review_count','brand_name']"
[BRANCH] "I always skip straight to the ingredients list and reviews – that's what really matters for my skin."
[SYNTHETIC] "Choose the detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages"
[SYNTHETIC] "I'd choose 15 min reading ingredient lists, 10 min reading text reviews, 5 min comparing prices."
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept, they would immediately look for the full ingredient list and scan for specific certifications (paraben-free, sulfate-free).

nodes.label: loyal-but-reasonable-switcher
nodes.description: Very loyal to familiar brands (like Dove) but will actively explore alternatives if prices increase significantly or if a trusted source recommends a change.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "How loyal are you to personal care brands? Very_loyal"
[REAL] "If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? Look_alternatives"
[REAL] "Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. What triggered the switch? Were you satisfied? Got it for free, otherwise loyal to Dove."
[SYNTHETIC] "Try the D2C brand recommended by my friend to explore alternatives."
nodes.predictive_value: If their current brand raises prices by >30%, they will research alternatives but will need strong evidence (reviews/trusted rec) to switch.

nodes.label: friends-family-first-validator
nodes.description: Trusts recommendations from friends and family above all other sources, including influencers and advertisements.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? ... Friends and family: 4"
[REAL] "Where do you typically discover new personal care products? Pick your top 3: ['youtube','friends_family','instagram']"
[SYNTHETIC] "Choose my close friend's recommendation"
[SYNTHETIC] "I'd allocate 20 points to what friends/family recommend vs 10 to influencers."
nodes.predictive_value: A product endorsed by a close friend or family member would overcome initial skepticism and trigger serious consideration.

nodes.label: natural-as-toxic-free-definer
nodes.description: Defines 'natural' specifically as the absence of carcinogenic or toxic chemicals, not as Ayurvedic or plant-based.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "natural means it free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin"
[REAL] "Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. Paraben_free,ph_balanced,sulfate_free,cruelty_free"
[REAL] "What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ['india_specific','intense_moisture','natural']"
nodes.predictive_value: Will respond strongly to claims like 'free from carcinogens' or 'non-toxic' but may be indifferent to general 'Ayurvedic' or 'plant-based' claims.

nodes.label: indian-brand-preferrer
nodes.description: Actively prefers Indian personal care brands over international ones due to perceived better value, accessibility, and credibility.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Indian personal care brands are performing well Ñ appropriately priced, easy to access and restock. Don't enjoy international brands as they lack credibility"
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy 'Made in India' at ₹249 over imported at ₹449."
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the popular Indian brand I've seen ads for over an international brand."
nodes.predictive_value: A new Indian brand with a clear value proposition will capture attention more easily than an international one, even with similar features.

nodes.label: zero-premium-for-values
nodes.description: Unwilling to pay any extra price for values like sustainability, Ayurvedic ingredients, clinical testing, or cruelty-free alone.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0','Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0','Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation':'0','Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'0','Made in India':'0'}"
[SYNTHETIC] "₹0 — these claims don't influence what I'm willing to pay."
[SYNTHETIC] "Skip it since I'm paying for the product, not the bottle."
nodes.predictive_value: Ethical or value-based marketing (eco-friendly, cruelty-free) will not justify a higher price point unless paired with superior core benefits.

nodes.label: online-review-volume-trumps-rating
nodes.description: Trusts products with a high volume of reviews (thousands) more than those with a higher average rating but fewer reviews.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? Brand A"
[SYNTHETIC] "The one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average"
[SYNTHETIC] "Pick the one with 12,000+ ratings and 4.3 stars for reliability."
nodes.predictive_value: A new product with a 4.8 rating but only 50 reviews will be viewed with suspicion compared to an established 4.2-rated product with 10,000+ reviews.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-for-urgent-needs-only
nodes.description: Uses quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto) for immediate replenishment when almost out, but prefers Amazon/Nykaa for selection and research.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? Yes, use quick commerce. If limited selection, look on Amazon or Nykaa"
[SYNTHETIC] "Order from Blinkit at ₹299 for 10-minute delivery since I'm almost out."
[SYNTHETIC] "Pick from Zepto’s limited range for instant delivery."
nodes.predictive_value: Will not discover or experiment with new brands on quick commerce; uses it as a convenience channel for known products.

nodes.label: lightweight-gentle-moisturizing-seeker
nodes.description: Seeks body washes that are lightweight, mild, gentle on skin (including face), and provide a moisturized post-wash feel.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "What texture do you prefer in a body wash? Lightweight"
[REAL] "What do you like MOST about your current body wash? (Top 1-2 things) Lightweight, can use on face, cleans effectively, mild"
[REAL] "What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? Moisturized"
[REAL] "What specific benefits or features would you expect from a body wash designed for Indian skin and hard water? Lightweight, mild, rinses off easily, moisturizes, cleans, gentle on skin"
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts emphasizing 'lightweight', 'gentle enough for face', and '24hr moisturization' will resonate more than those focusing on fragrance or lather.

nodes.label: doctor-recommendation-switch-trigger
nodes.description: A recommendation from a dermatologist or doctor is a top trigger to consider switching from a loyal brand.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ['doctor_rec','free_sample']"
[REAL] "What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: ['Expert_review','ingredient_breakdown']"
[SYNTHETIC] "Research more dermatologists' opinions before deciding."
nodes.predictive_value: An ad or article featuring a credible dermatologist's endorsement would be highly effective in capturing their attention and driving trial.

[SYNTHETIC] "Disagree but watch the full video to understand their reasoning."

nodes.label: price-conscious-compromiser
nodes.description: Willing to compromise on size, format, or even brand to stay within a comfortable price range (₹200-₹350), but not on core ingredient/quality needs.

[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249 since price matters more."

nodes.label: cautious-novelty-trier-via-samples

[SYNTHETIC] "Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size."
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the sachet to try — perfect low-risk trial."
[SYNTHETIC] "Try the sample but it won't change my loyalty to Brand A."
nodes.predictive_value: A free sample strategy or a ₹1 trial offer will be far more effective in driving first-time trial than a discount on a full-size product.

nodes.label: honest-influencer-trust-but-verify
nodes.description: Trusts influencers perceived as genuine and not doing PR (like Ankush Bahuguna) but still cross-checks their recommendations with other sources.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "What is it about Ankush Bahuguna that makes you trust his opinions on personal care products? Honest, genuine, uses few products so clearly not PR, very honest reviews"
[SYNTHETIC] "Influencer A influences more due to expert credibility despite smaller following."
[SYNTHETIC] "Use it but cross-check with reviews to ensure reliability."
nodes.predictive_value: An influencer review can create initial interest, but they will still visit Amazon/Nykaa to read user reviews before purchasing.

nodes.label: india-specific-skin-claim-responder
nodes.description: Highly responsive to claims about products being formulated specifically for Indian skin types or hard water conditions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? {'rating':5,'explanation':'yes'}"
[REAL] "What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ['india_specific','intense_moisture','natural']"
[SYNTHETIC] "Would try it, driven mainly by the India-specific tribal recipe claim."
nodes.predictive_value: A product line explicitly marketed as 'For Indian Skin' or 'Hard Water Formula' will immediately capture their attention and interest.

nodes.label: skeptical-of-greenwashing
nodes.description: Questions environmental or natural claims and seeks to verify them through ingredient lists or independent research.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Research the claim myself before deciding to continue or stop buying."
[SYNTHETIC] "Research what 'natural' legally means in Indian cosmetics regulation for clarity."
[SYNTHETIC] "Make me compare this breakdown with other brands to see if marketing costs are justified."
[SYNTHETIC] "Feel cheated but probably still buy the ₹299 one for its branding/fragrance."
nodes.predictive_value: Vague 'eco-friendly' or 'clean beauty' claims without transparent ingredient backing will be met with suspicion, not admiration.

nodes.label: functional-over-experiential-prioritizer
nodes.description: Prioritizes functional benefits (cleansing, moisturizing, gentleness) over experiential aspects (luxury fragrance, novel textures, packaging aesthetics).
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Perfect body wash: ... fragrance not important, lightweight gentle formulation, cleans and moisturizes"
[REAL] "How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? Nice_to_have"
[SYNTHETIC] "Would not buy primarily for the experience at ₹549, too expensive for average product."
[SYNTHETIC] "Skip it — I'm paying for the product, not the bottle."
nodes.predictive_value: Ads focusing on sensory pleasure or packaging design will be less effective than those demonstrating the product's core functional performance and ingredients.

nodes.label: multi-platform-price-review-checker
nodes.description: Routinely checks prices and reviews across multiple platforms (Amazon, Nykaa, quick commerce) before finalizing a purchase, even in-store.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Search for usual brand on another app to see if cheaper, then decide."
[SYNTHETIC] "Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first."
[SYNTHETIC] "Check another platform for more authentic reviews."
[SYNTHETIC] "Scan the product in the Nykaa app and read reviews right there."
nodes.predictive_value: In-store promotions or displays will often trigger a mobile research session rather than an immediate purchase.

nodes.label: P05_T018
nodes.identity_summary: A pragmatic yet loyal Dove user who prioritizes ingredients and online reviews over marketing claims. While fun-loving and mood-dependent in their grooming, they exhibit strong research habits—checking ingredient lists, comparing multiple sources, and valuing expert/friend opinions. They prefer Indian brands for better value but won't pay premiums for values; their loyalty is conditional on price, trusted recommendations, or free samples.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-scrutinizer-before-purchase
nodes.description: Prioritizes examining the ingredient list and seeks clear explanations of what each component does before making a purchase decision.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q11: When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: ingredients,brand,price,recommendation,expert,reviews,packaging,fragrance
[REAL] Q24: When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: ingredients,review_count,brand_name

[SYNTHETIC] Q112: You're comparing two body washes on Amazon India. One listing has a detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages, the other has a 2-minute brand video showing the product in use. If you could only view one before deciding, which do you choose? A: Detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages
[SYNTHETIC] Q149: You have 100 points to allocate across these activities that you'd do BEFORE buying a body wash for the first time... A: Checking the ingredient list 35, Reading online reviews 30
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would immediately seek the complete ingredient list and would engage more with content explaining each ingredient's function in simple terms.

nodes.label: multi-source-online-review-validator
nodes.description: Doesn't trust reviews on a single platform; cross-checks across Amazon, Nykaa, YouTube, and Reddit to form a balanced opinion.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q59: Do you read online reviews before buying personal care products? Where? A: Reddit,youtube,amazon,nykaa
[SYNTHETIC] Q113: A body wash you're interested in has conflicting information... What do you do? A: C) Search for more reviews on other platforms

[SYNTHETIC] Q119: You find a body wash on Nykaa that has 4.5 stars but all the top reviews seem overly positive and possibly incentivized. Do you... A: Check another platform for more authentic reviews

[SYNTHETIC] Q126: You notice a body wash on Zepto has a 'Bestseller' tag but no detailed description or reviews on the app. Would you... A: Search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto
nodes.predictive_value: When considering a new product, this person would be skeptical of high ratings on one platform alone and would likely delay purchase until they've verified reviews across at least 2-3 different sites.

nodes.label: loyal-until-price-increase-or-trusted-recommendation
nodes.description: Shows strong loyalty to familiar brands (especially Dove) but will actively explore alternatives if prices rise significantly or if a trusted personal source recommends something new.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q16: How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Very_loyal
[REAL] Q19: When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Repurchase_same
[REAL] Q26: If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives

[BRANCH] Q64: You've been using a ₹199 body wash for a year and are satisfied. A colleague at work casually mentions your body wash is 'the budget option' and recommends their ₹499 one... A: I'd be curious and look up the ₹499 option
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad for their current brand, they'd likely ignore it unless it highlights a price drop. However, a targeted recommendation from a trusted friend or colleague could successfully trigger a brand switch.

nodes.label: prefers-indian-brands-for-value-and-accessibility
nodes.description: Actively prefers Indian personal care brands over international ones, citing better pricing, easier access, and perceived higher credibility for the Indian market.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q14: How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Indian personal care brands are performing well — appropriately priced, easy to access and restock. Don't enjoy international brands as they lack credibility

[SYNTHETIC] Q83: A body wash brand offers two identical products with different labels: one says 'Made in India' priced at ₹249, the other says 'Imported from Korea' priced at ₹449. Same size, similar ingredients. Which are you more likely to buy? A: Buy the 'Made in India' option at ₹249
[SYNTHETIC] Q161: If you had to allocate 10 'trust points' across these body wash brand types... A: Legacy Indian brands 4, New-age Indian D2C brands 3
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a new product concept, messaging that emphasizes 'Made in India,' 'formulated for Indian skin/water,' or 'easy availability across India' would significantly increase engagement and purchase intent.

nodes.label: values-natural-but-defines-it-as-toxin-free
nodes.description: Rates 'natural' as very important (5/5), but defines it specifically as the absence of toxic or carcinogenic chemicals rather than Ayurvedic or plant-based ingredients.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q12: How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? And what does 'natural' mean to you in your own words? A: {"rating":5,"explanation":"natural means it free of all toxics that will have carcinogenic effect on my face, body or skin"}
[REAL] Q13: Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Paraben_free,ph_balanced,sulfate_free,cruelty_free
